heroHeadline: Isaacman at House Appropriations CJS; Heat Shield Inspection Week 3; Senate Hearing Tomorrow
heroSubtitle: Apr 27: Twenty-seven days post-splashdown, NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman testified before the House Appropriations Commerce, Justice, Science (CJS) Subcommittee at 3:30 PM ET — the committee that writes the actual FY2027 funding bill, distinct from the April 22 House Science Committee session. Isaacman again defended the $18.8B FY2027 request (23% overall cut, 47% Science Mission Directorate reduction), citing Artemis II's successful April 1–10 crewed lunar flyby. A companion Senate CJS Appropriations hearing is scheduled for April 28. At KSC's Multi-Payload Processing Facility, the formal 30-day Orion heat shield inspection enters week three — Marshall Space Flight Center X-ray scans and Avcoat sample extractions are underway; results expected ~May 10 will define the Artemis IV 2028 crewed lunar landing timeline. Starship V3 Flight 12 remains on track for a May orbital demonstration pending FAA license at Starbase Pad 2. The Artemis II crew continues physiological reconditioning at JSC.
footerNote: Data sourced from NASA.gov, OIG Reports, SpaceNews, Ars Technica, Congressional appropriations. Tier 1: NASA/ESA/Official. Tier 2: SpaceNews/Ars Technica/Reuters. Tier 3: Planetary Society/AIAA.
